title: 10 best herbal decoctions for inflamed gums
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for inflamed gums are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water, which helps to soothe and calm irritated gums. These decoctions work by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria that cause infection, and promoting healing. Examples of herbal decoctions that can help with inflamed gums include chamomile, peppermint, and sage teas. By using these decoctions, individuals can experience relief from pain, swelling, and bleeding associated with inflamed gums, allowing them to enjoy a healthier, more comfortable smile and improved oral function.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, particularly the triterpenoid saponins. These compounds exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, effectively reducing swelling and pain in the gum tissue. Additionally, the decoction's flavonoids and phenolic acids help to stabilize the oral microbiome, preventing the overgrowth of pathogenic bacteria that can contribute to inflamed gums. By addressing both inflammation and microbial imbalance, Kansas coneflower decoctions offer a holistic approach to promoting healthy, resilient gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, which help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antibacterial activity: The decoction has been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties against certain oral bacteria, including Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis, which are known to contribute to gum inflammation and disease.

Wound-healing and tissue repair: Kansas coneflower contains bioactive compounds that stimulate collagen synthesis and enhance wound healing, promoting the repair of damaged gum tissue and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically echinacoside and isoechinacoside, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in inflamed gums.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides present in Echinacea angustifolia have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate inflammation and promote healing in inflamed gum tissue.

Phenylethyl esters (Echinacosides): Echinacosides, a type of phenylethyl ester, have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which may help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in echinacoside and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums.
Barks: They have been used to make decoctions that help to soothe and calm inflamed gums due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall with scissors or a sharp knife.
Clean and dry the harvested roots to remove dirt and debris with a paper towel.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces to increase their surface area using a sharp knife.
Steep 2-3 teaspoons of the chopped root pieces in 1 quart of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in sage work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, providing relief from painful inflammation. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties help combat the underlying causes of gum disease, such as bacteria and plaque buildup. By soothing and healing the affected areas, sage decoctions promote a healthy, balanced oral environment, reducing inflammation and promoting overall dental health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and camphor, help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation by inhibiting the growth of unwanted microorganisms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in sage decoctions possess anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and gum disease.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.
Thymol: This monoterpene has antimicrobial properties, which help to control the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to gum inflammation.
Salvianolic acid: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation, protect against oxidative stress, and promote healing of inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most used part of Salvia officinalis to make decoctions for inflamed gums due to their high content of bioactive compounds, particularly tannins and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems are also used for decoctions, as they contain similar bioactive compounds to the leaves, providing additional anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects to soothe inflamed gums.

Barks: The barks of Salvia officinalis are used in decoctions for inflamed gums due to their rich content of salvinolic acids and other compounds, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated, using scissors or pinch them off.
Chop 2-3 teaspoons of fresh leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves with 1 cup of boiling water, reduce heat to a simmer.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength of decoction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint has been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing inflamed gums. Additionally, peppermint's antibacterial qualities help combat the underlying infection that may be causing the inflammation. By reducing redness, swelling, and pain, peppermint decoctions provide relief from gum inflammation, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antimicrobial: The volatile oils in peppermint decoction, including limonene and menthone, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections and inflammation.
Antioxidant: Peppermint's antioxidant properties help protect gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol, a terpene found in peppermint oil, helps reduce inflammation and ease pain in the gums by numbing the affected area and decreasing the sensation of pain.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and combat oxidative stress in inflamed gums.
Eugenol: Eugenol, a phenolic compound, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help ease pain and reduce inflammation in the gums, making it an effective ingredient in oral care products.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Mentha x piperita in decoctions for inflamed gums, as they contain menthol and menthone that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used in decoctions, as they contribute to the overall menthol content and provide additional anti-inflammatory benefits.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are sometimes used in decoctions, as they contain a higher concentration of menthol and other bioactive compounds that can aid in reducing gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of mentha x piperita in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Chop 1/4 cup of fresh leaves into fine fragments using a sharp knife for efficient infusion.
Combine the chopped leaves with 1 quart of boiling water in a heat-resistant container for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Store the cooled decoction in a glass container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The decoction's bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby decreasing gum inflammation. Additionally, germander's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that can contribute to gum disease, promoting a healthy oral environment and supporting the body's natural defense against infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Germander decoctions contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and triterpenes, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant properties: The decoction's antioxidant properties help protect the gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and tissue degeneration in inflamed gums.

Antimicrobial activity: Germander decoctions have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi that can cause periodontal diseases, helping to reduce the severity of gum infections and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, alleviating symptoms of inflamed gums.
Teucrin A: A sesquiterpene lactone that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections causing gum inflammation.
Teucrin B: A sesquiterpene lactone that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential in reducing gum inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Teucrium chamaedrys are used to make decoctions for inflamed gums due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Teucrium chamaedrys are used to make decoctions for inflamed gums due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight infections.

Leaves (again, since only 3 could be included in the list, and leaves were the only part that fit more than one criteria): The leaves of Teucrium chamaedrys are also used to make decoctions for inflamed gums due to their astringent properties, which help to reduce bleeding and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh or dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves in sufficient quantity for decoction preparation approximately 25 grams.
Chop the collected leaves into smaller pieces to enhance their release of bioactive compounds during decoction.
Combine the chopped teucrium chamaedrys leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The decoction's active compound, methyl salicylate, is a natural analog of aspirin that effectively reduces inflammation and relieves pain. Additionally, wintergreen's antiseptic properties help to combat bacterial growth and promote healing in the affected areas. By drinking these decoctions regularly, individuals can experience significant relief from inflamed gums and maintain good oral health.
remedy_properties: Methyl salicylate: Wintergreen's main active compound, methyl salicylate, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.

Flavonoids: Wintergreen contains flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help protect gum tissues from damage and reduce inflammation.

Tannins: The tannins present in wintergreen decoctions can help constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and swelling in inflamed gums, while also providing a mild astringent effect to soothe sore gums.
remedy_constituents: Gaultherin: A sesquiterpene lignan: Gaultherin has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections in inflamed gums.

Ascorbic acid (Vitamin C): A phenolic antioxidant: Ascorbic acid has strong antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and tissue repair.
Gentiopicroside: A phenolic iridoid glycoside: Gentiopicroside has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce gum inflammation.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to soothe inflamed gums.
Barks: Contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in late summer when they are mature and free of contamination.
Cut the harvested plant material into small pieces and weigh out 30 grams for every 250 milliliters of water.
Combine the chopped plant material with 250 milliliters of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove the plant material and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The decoction's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce swelling and ease pain, while its antiseptic compounds combat the bacteria that cause gum infections. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect against further damage and promote healthy tissue repair. remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Marigold decoction's antioxidant property helps to reduce inflammation by neutralizing free radicals that can damage gum tissues, thus reducing gum swelling and pain.
Anti-inflammatory: The decoction's anti-inflammatory property reduces inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds, thereby relieving discomfort and bleeding.

remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
Flavonoids (especially Quercetin): Quercetin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate inflammation, reduce oxidative stress, and promote healing of inflamed gums.
Sesquiterpenes (especially Calendulin): Calendulin has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce bacterial growth, inflammation, and pain associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help reduce inflammation and fight infection.
Stems: As they contain bioactive compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 flowers of calendula officinalis in the morning after the dew has dried and the sun has risen.
Rinse the flowers thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the cleaned flowers with 1 liter of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes to release the active compounds from the flowers.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to obtain the decoction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood decoctions helps with inflamed gums because it has potent anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The decoction's bioactive compounds, such as thujone and camphor, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby diminishing inflammation and promoting healing. Additionally, wormwood's antimicrobial properties help combat periodontal pathogens, further contributing to a reduction in gum inflammation and promoting overall oral health.

Tannins: The tannin-rich extracts of wormwood decoctions have astringent and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce gum bleeding and prevent bacterial growth that may contribute to inflamed gums.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: This sesquiterpene acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.
Artemisinin: This sesquiterpene lactone exhibits strong antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the underlying bacterial or fungal infections causing gum inflammation.
Flavonoids (specifically, apigenin and luteolin): These phenolic compounds possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe and protect the inflamed gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.
Stems: The stems are used for their astringent properties, which help tighten the gums and reduce inflammation.
Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and heal inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 ounces of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and stems for decoction preparation.
Combine the gathered herb with 32 ounces of boiling water in a large pot for steeping.
Allow the herbal mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes for optimal extraction of properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Let the strained decoction cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gum tissue. Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums.

remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain essential oils and compounds like thymol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers or 2 ounces of dried leaves and flowers.
Rinse the thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers with cool water to remove impurities.
Combine the thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used in decoctions for inflamed gums due to their high menthol content, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used in decoctions for inflamed gums because of their antimicrobial properties, which help to combat bacterial infections that can cause inflammation.
Roots: Eucalyptus globulus roots are used in decoctions for inflamed gums due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh or dried leaves of eucalyptus globulus and clean them thoroughly with cold running water.
Combine the prepared eucalyptus globulus leaves with 1 liter of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool completely and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in rosemary, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Additionally, rosemary's antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum disease. When consumed as a decoction, rosemary's active ingredients are absorbed into the bloodstream, reaching the gums to provide relief from inflammation and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Rosemary's antibacterial compounds, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation, reducing the risk of further infection and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary contains anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor and bornyl acetate, which reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating discomfort.

Stems: Stems are used for decoctions as they contain rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain in inflamed gums.
Buds: Buds are used for decoctions due to their high concentration of essential oils, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and place them in a clean glass container.
Heat 1 cup of water to boiling point over medium heat for 5 minutes.
Add the dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water and steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the rosmarinus officinalis decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Discard the solids and drink the decoction immediately while it is still warm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain. The plant's compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxin, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, thus reducing gum inflammation. Additionally, dandelion's antibacterial properties help combat periodontal bacteria that contribute to gum disease. By consuming dandelion decoctions, individuals can promote healthy gums, reduce bleeding, and alleviate discomfort associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion's decoction contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion decoction, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue, promoting healthy gum growth and reducing oxidative stress.

Astringent properties: Dandelion's astringent compounds, like taraxanthin, help reduce bleeding and inflammation by contracting the gums and mucous membranes, thereby stopping bleeding and soothing the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in inflamed gums, making them a popular choice for natural gum care.
Fruits: The fruit of the dandelion plant contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and roots, rinsing them thoroughly with cold water.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale with 250ml boiling distilled water, letting it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Add 10 drops of tea tree essential oil to the liquid decoction and stir well, allowing it to cool slightly.
Use the finished decoction within 24 hours, applying it directly to inflamed gums using a cotton swab for 3-5 minutes twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin and licoricidin, which have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth. These compounds work by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. As a result, licorice decoctions can help to soothe and calm inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in licorice decoctions help neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to gum tissue, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to gum disease.
Soothing demulcent effect: Licorice root's mucilage content forms a protective barrier on the gums' surface, soothing and calming irritated tissues, reducing discomfort, and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties, the roots help soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Rhyzomes: Rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, the rhyzomes have natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that combat gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root from a trusted source and rinse it with distilled water.
Combine the harvested root with 4 cups of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container and allow it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Bring the strained liquid to a boil again in a saucepan, then reduce the heat and simmer for an additional 10 minutes to enhance bioavailability.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before using it as a mouthwash or gargling agent twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Prairie toothpick decoctions helps with inflamed gums because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that specifically target the affected areas. The decoction's unique blend of plant extracts, such as chamomile and calendula, soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing swelling and redness. As the natural antibacterial properties work to eliminate harmful oral bacteria, the decoction also promotes healthy gum growth and strengthens tooth attachment, promoting overall oral health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Plants like Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis) and Sage (Salvia officinalis) contain compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to gum inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) and Peppermint (Mentha piperita) contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Antioxidant activity: Plants like Echinacea (Echinacea spp.) and Green Tea (Camellia sinensis) are rich in antioxidants that can help protect the gum tissue from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks have been traditionally used to treat gum inflammation due to their ability to reduce bleeding and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh roots of zanthoxylum americanum and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least seven days.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried root material and combine it with two cups of filtered water in a medium saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce the heat to low and simmer for fifteen minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool at room temperature before refrigerating it for up to three days or freezing for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The decoction's antimicrobial compounds also combat bacteria that can exacerbate gum inflammation, promoting a healthy mouth environment. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in inflamed gums.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with inflamed gums.
Vasoconstrictive properties: Yarrow's ability to constrict blood vessels helps reduce bleeding and gum inflammation by decreasing blood flow to the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation.
Buds: Yarrow buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce swelling and pain associated with inflamed gums.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried achillea millefolium herb per eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured herb and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

The plantain's flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids work together to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, the decoction's antimicrobial compounds help eliminate the bacteria responsible for the infection, promoting a healthy balance in the oral microbiome. As a result, plantain decoctions can provide effective relief from inflamed gums, reducing redness, bleeding, and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Plantain's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aucubin and cinnamic acid, reduce swelling and redness in inflamed gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant property: Plantain's antioxidant properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammation and gum disease.
Wound-healing property: Plantain's wound-healing compounds, such as allantoin and vitamin K, promote the growth of new tissues and accelerate the healing process for inflamed and damaged gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease pain in inflamed gums.
Barks: The barks are rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding in inflamed gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the washed leaves into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Combine one tablespoon of chopped plantago major with two cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with inflamed gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning for optimal potency and freshness.
Rinse 10-15 grams of the plant material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed plant material with 500ml of boiling water in a stainless steel pot, then reduce heat to simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before transferring it to an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for storage in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean 20-30 grams of fresh nettle leaves and stems from a pesticide-free area.
Combine the harvested nettle material with 1 liter of boiling water in a large pot.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes to allow for maximum extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried hydrangea arborescens roots into a clean glass container or tea infuser.
Bring four cups of pure water to boil in a saucepan on high heat for five minutes.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the hydrangea arborescens in the boiling water for 30 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.

remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried myrrhis odorata herb into a clean glass container.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the container and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Discard the solids and reserve the liquid decoction in an amber-colored glass bottle.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days prior to use.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflamed gums that are treated with herbal decoctions are various oral health issues, such as mouth ulcers, thrush, and tonsillitis. Herbal remedies like goldenseal, echinacea, and tea tree oil can help soothe irritated mucous membranes, reduce swelling, and combat bacterial infections. These natural treatments can also alleviate symptoms of conditions like sinusitis, bronchitis, and cystitis, which often manifest in the mouth and throat.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with inflamed gums is a blend of Aloe Vera, Echinacea, and Sage. Aloe Vera soothes and calms the gums, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Echinacea enhances the body's immune response, fighting off the underlying infection causing the inflammation. Sage, with its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helps to reduce swelling and prevent further infection. Together, these decoctions create a powerful and natural remedy for inflamed gums, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of medicinal food, herbal decoctions can be beneficial for inflamed gums due to their anti-inflammatory properties. The study found that aqueous extracts from 20 medicinal herbs commonly used in the Hispanic culture, including laurel tea, exhibited significant anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting the central signaling pathway of the inflammatory response. Specifically, laurel tea extract was found to be an especially potent inhibitor of NF-kappaB-dependent cyclooxygenase-2 gene expression and prostaglandin E(2) production, which may help in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of inflamed gums.
